title: Adhikari aide killing: CBI conducts searches at multiple locations
content: The search operation was conducted late at night on May 15 and continued till the early hours of May 16

title: Census 2027: Field visits for house-listing, household data collection begin in Jharkhand
content: The exercise will run till June 14, 2026, during which trained enumerators will undertake house-to-house visits

title: Bhojshala Case: What did the High Court decide?
content: The Madhya Pradesh High Court has ruled on the long-standing Bhojshala-Kamal Maula dispute, declaring the site a Hindu temple and granting full worship rights. The verdict has sparked reactions from both sides and may now head to the Supreme Court. Here’s a detailed breakdown of the case and why it matters.

title: PM Modi in Netherlands LIVE: Prime Minister lauds Dutch coach’s role in Indian women’s hockey success
content: The Prime Minister said he would hold talks with Dutch PM Jetten and call on King Willem-Alexander and Queen Maxima

title: Delhi Govt to soon notify city logistics plan: CM Rekha Gupta
content: The Chief Minister said the national capital has secured a place in the highest "Exemplary" category in the country's Logistics Ease Across Different States 2025 Index

title: Fourteen illegal immigrants prevented from entering India: Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma
content: ''A group of 14 illegals were identified and prevented from entering into India by brave jawans of @assampolice and @BSF_India,'' the CM posted on X on May 15

title: Telangana HC denies pre-arrest bail to Bandi Sanjay’s son, reserves order
content: Justice T. Madhavi Devi of the HC, who heard contentions of the parties from 9 p.m. to close to midnight, said that she was “not inclined to grant any interim order at this stage”

title: T.N. Governor cuts security convoy size after PM Modi’s austerity appeal
content: Until now, 10 security vehicles accompanied the Governor when he participated in government and private functions; the number has now been reduced to four

title: Retired judge, son booked for dowry harassment after daughter-in-law found dead
content: The victim’s family has accused her husband and mother-in-law of mentally harassing and misbehaving with her and making financial demands.

title: UN event highlights India-Oman maritime ties through maiden voyage of stitched sail vessel
content: The Indian Ambassador to the UN Parvathaneni Harish underlined that India and Oman share a maritime relationship of peaceful trade and commerce that goes back centuries

title: Maharashtra Govt mandates Marathi for revenue hearings and orders
content: The new SoP also makes it mandatory for all revenue officers to conduct hearings at least twice a week, preferably on Tuesdays and Fridays, while adjournments will be allowed only once

title: Supreme Court-monitored judicial probe needed over leakage of NEET examination papers: CWC member Gidugu Rudra Raju
content: Speaking to The Hindu, Gidugu Rudra Raju said the 22,05,035 students who appeared for examinations in 5,432 centres had become victims with cancellation of NEET examinations

title: Tamil Nadu govt. may gradually increase women’s rights grant
content: The amount may initially be increased from ₹1,000 to ₹1,500,  given the State’s financial situation, says a government representative

title: CPI(M) extended support to TVK to prevent BJP’s ‘backdoor rule’: P. Shanmugam
content: “Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay has promised good governance. Our support will depend on the performance of the government,” the CPI(M) State secretary said

title: BRICS is evolving with new members; differences will take time to resolve: Brazil FM Mauro Vieira
content: UAE-Iran tensions over the war hold up consensus at BRICS FM meet; could spell trouble for BRICS Summit in September

title: Governor’s secretariat writes to Karnataka government on timely conduct of Bengaluru civic elections
content: The Governor’s secretariat requested the Chief Secretary to take necessary steps to ensure that the SEC receives adequate financial and logistical support for holding the elections without delay

title: Bengal Government orders re-verification of caste certificates issued since 2011
content: Allegations of corruption and irregularities in the issuance of caste certificates had repeatedly surfaced during the previous government's tenure

title: Church in Ennore sets up pastoral desk for transgender people
content: The desk will cater to trans persons, particularly in Chennai, and aims to address the community’s spiritual needs, as well as concerns related to employment and mental health counselling.

title: Madras High Court appoints industrialist Vijayakumar Reddy as interim administrator of Chettinad Vidyashram school
content: Justice N. Senthilkumar passes the order following a few applications filed by industrialist M.A.M.R. Muthiah, his wife Geetha Muthiah, and five others

title: Gunmen open fire at furniture mall in Pimpri Chinchwad; police suspect Bishnoi gang link
content: “The assailants fired multiple rounds at the AJ Furniture Mall in the Ravet area at around 9 p.m. on May 15,” a police official said

title: Woman lawyer stabbing case: Supreme Court asks police to conclude probe quickly, ensure her protection
content: According to the allegations, the victim, a practising advocate at the Karkardooma district court, was stabbed with a sword by her husband

title: Forensic tests confirm rapper Vedan possessed original leopard tooth illegally
content: “The pendant was sent to the Central Forensic Lab in Kolkata in order to confirm whether it is original leopard tooth. We have received the confirmation that it is genuine,” officials said

title: How the humble Kerala stew is finally having its moment
content: The Kerala stew, usually an accompaniment to appam or bread, is fast emerging as a standalone dish with restaurants, cloud kitchens and a food truck dedicated to it

title: Lorry runs over women labourers in Kakinada; four killed, two critical
content: The incident occurred when a group of six women were on their way to attend work under the VB-G RAM G, formerly known as MGNREGA

title: NIA chargesheets ‘mastermind,’ four others in Cambodia-linked ‘cyber slavery’ case
content: The syndicate was involved in luring Indian youth to Cambodia on the pretext of legitimate jobs and lucrative salaries, as part of an organised human trafficking racket

title: Supreme Court flags ‘slow’ trial in murder case against actor Darshan; asks him to revive bail plea after a year
content: The top court observed that the trial in the 2024 Renukaswamy murder case was moving at an “extremely slow” pace with only 10 witnesses examined in seven months

title: Meghalaya: Looking beyond the grape
content: How the state’s winemakers are making the shift from traditional home-brewing to commercial production, spurred on by market demand for its fruit wines and an enabling government policy

title: The day World War II reached Andhra Pradesh: echoes of forgotten battle still linger across Vizag
content: From pillboxes that emerge during low tide to coastal battery, wartime plaques and abandoned fortifications, Visakhapatnam continues to carry traces of World War II beneath its modern landscape

title: Mumbai water cut: BMC warns of criminal action against electric pump users
content: Municipal Commissioner Abhijit Bangar on May 15, directed civic officials to intensify action against illegal practices, and said repeat offenders would also face disconnection of water supply

title: MIT-Bengaluru taps CMTI facility to prepare future-focused semiconductor talent pipeline
content: The programme offered a group of students from MIT Bengaluru under-graduate students direct exposure to semiconductor fabrication processes that are typically inaccessible at the undergraduate level in India

title: Police raid 15 locations in J&K's Baramulla; seize 'incriminating material' linked to banned outfit
content: Search operations were carried out at more than 15 locations including Jamia Qadeem, Naseem Bagh, Krankshivan, Tarzoo, Amargarh, Warpora, Bomai, Boitingoo and other areas of Sopore in north Kashmir

title: Bay system to trigger wet spell, ease heat in Tamil Nadu for next few days
content: Many parts of the State may receive heavy to moderate rainfall on Saturday; weather system may influence wet conditions till May 19, says RMC chief

title: After 50 years, Karnataka pushes to bring Lakkundi artefacts home from Delhi
content: The artefacts were formally handed over in October 1976 by D.K. Hebboor and T.K. Nayak to then Prime Minister Indira Gandhi, following which they were placed in the custody of the National Museum

title: Transport charge between textile hubs up by 50%, commercial LPG hike further raises dyeing costs in Karnataka
content: While petrol and diesel prices were increased up by over ₹3 per litre on May 15, traders fear a further hike in the coming months

title: Why IUML firmly backed V.D. Satheesan for Kerala Chief Minister
content: Political analysts say the former Leader of the Opposition cultivated strong acceptance among Muslims and many see him as “one among us”

title: AIADMK split: Velumani faction willing to hold talks with Palaniswami
content: Referring to Mr. Palaniswami’s statement that he was ready to make any sacrifice, Mr. Velumani said that the AIADMK general secretary should invite his faction for a discussion, at least once

title: Aequs, IIT-D open advanced R&D centre for materials science, manufacturing innovation in Dharwad
content: In addition to research, the facility also conducts hands-on training programmes for engineers, researchers, and students, strengthening capabilities in advanced analysis of metals and non-metals

title: Mamata asks Trinamool Congress poll candidates to rebuild party; says dissenters free to leave
content: At a meeting with the Trinamool Congress' contesting candidates, she said the organisation would rise again despite suffering a crushing defeat in the recently concluded Assembly elections

title: Kerala HC permits transgender person to approach any fertility clinic to freeze eggs
content: Kerala High Court permits transgender man to freeze eggs, enhancing reproductive rights and healthcare access.

title: After a long gap, farming to be resumed on abandoned lands of  Periyar Tiger Reserve
content: Project aims to provide financial stability to the tribal community while reducing instances of human-wildlife conflicts

title: Visakhapatnam zoo summer camp to begin from May 19
content: IGZP to host five-day summer camp with wildlife learning, birdwatching and interactive activities for children

title: APSPDCL to provide prepaid smart meters for government services from June 1
content: A whopping 1.17 lakh services are likely to be brought under this prepaid metering system, which includes 91,306 single-phase and 26,507 three-phase services

title: PM Modi arrives in Netherlands on second leg of five-nation tour
content: Mr. Modi said he would hold talks with Prime Minister Jetten and call on King Willem-Alexander and Queen Maxima

title: Jammu and Kashmir CM Omar Abdullah approves ₹93 crore power infrastructure projects
content: The approval of these projects was accorded during the sixth meeting of the Council of Ministers, the J&K CM said

title: After Trump, Putin to visit China's Xi Jinping on May 19 for two days
content: Russian President Putin is set for a significant two-day visit to China, commencing May 19, to deepen strategic ties with President Xi Jinping. This follows closely on the heels of Donald Trump's high-profile trip to Beijing, which focused on Taiwan, trade, and Iran. The leaders will discuss strengthening their partnership and address key global issues, aiming to sign a joint declaration.

title: Look-out circular against Bandi Sanjay’s son: Cops form special teams in POCSO case
content: Cyberabad police have formed five special teams and issued a look-out circular for Bandi Sai Bhagirath, son of Union minister Bandi Sanjay Kumar, in a POCSO case. Investigators are using CDR and IP addresses to trace him. The case involves alleged sexual harassment and outraging modesty of a minor.

title: Virat Kohli reveals why RCB winning IPL title earlier 'wouldn't have felt even 5% the same'
content: Virat Kohli revealed the immense emotional weight lifted after Royal Challengers Bengaluru finally clinched their maiden IPL title after 18 years. He stated that a victory earlier in his career wouldn't have felt as special, highlighting the profound joy derived from overcoming decades of pressure and heartbreak alongside franchise icons and the city of Bengaluru.

title: 'Until Mars is colonised': Musk reveals SpaceX agreement to ensure he can't be fired
content: Elon Musk's compensation deal with SpaceX reportedly links his pay to colonizing Mars, potentially worth trillions. This structure aims to ensure his long-term leadership and focus on making humanity multi-planetary, rather than short-term financial gains. Musk confirmed the necessity of this approach, emphasizing the mission's immense value and difficulty.

title: 'People are forced to travel in bullock carts': Congress stages protest over fuel, milk price hike
content: Congress workers protested rising fuel and milk prices, with leaders arriving by bullock cart to highlight inflation's impact. Delhi Congress president Devendra Yadav criticized the government's handling of essential commodity costs, stating inflation has doubled since 2014. This demonstration follows a recent Rs 3 per litre hike in petrol and diesel prices across major cities.

title: NEET-UG paper leak row: Doctors' body calls for dissolution of NTA, files plea in Supreme Court
content: Doctors' body UDF has petitioned the Supreme Court to dissolve the National Testing Agency, citing a "catastrophic failure" in the NEET-UG 2026 exam's conduct due to alleged paper leaks. The plea also demands significant reforms for transparency and fairness in national entrance tests, aiming to prevent future irregularities.

title: Ex-Google CEO Eric Schmidt has message for engineers who are still coding the old way
content: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt declared the era of manual code writing is over, citing AI's explosive power. He believes programmers still using traditional methods are behind, as AI enables individuals to build powerful applications previously requiring large teams. This shift is causing a sell-off in software stocks, with companies needing to adapt quickly to survive.

title: J&K: Police officer shoots wife dead after a quarrel; attempts suicide
content: A probationary sub-inspector in Jammu and Kashmir's Kupwara district allegedly shot his wife dead following a late-night quarrel. The officer, identified as Deep Singh, then reportedly shot himself and is currently in critical condition undergoing treatment. The incident occurred at an SOG camp in the Kandi area.

title: Starbucks pours a bitter brew: Plans laying off 300 workers in $400 m restructuring
content: Starbucks is laying off approximately 300 US-based roles as it consolidates its office network and shutters several locations. This move is part of a broader strategy to achieve "durable, profitable growth" by reducing complexity and costs. The company expects additional international job cuts as it reviews its global support structure.

title: After Microsoft gave engineers access to Anthropic's Claude Code, company is canceling licenses
content: Microsoft is phasing out internal licenses for Anthropic's Claude Code AI tool, shifting developers to its own GitHub Copilot CLI platform. This strategic move, effective by the end of June, aims to streamline AI coding tools and align with Microsoft's engineering needs and financial year-end.

title: NCP goes all-Pawar: Sunetra takes charge as sons get key party roles; seniors sidelined
content: NCP president Sunetra Pawar's letter to the ECI, omitting key organizational posts for Praful Patel and Sunil Tatkare, has sparked speculation. Sources indicate Sunetra Pawar and her sons are seeking a meeting with Tatkare to clarify the issue. Pawar previously attributed similar omissions to technical mistakes.

title: India Vs Pakistan: How the two nations stack up when it comes to nuclear arsenals
content: India and Pakistan possess nearly identical nuclear arsenals, yet their doctrines diverge significantly. India adheres to 'No First Use' and aims for a minimum deterrent, while Pakistan's 'Full Spectrum Deterrence' counters India's conventional strength. India is expanding its nuclear triad, focusing on survivability and reach, while Pakistan emphasises tactical nuclear weapons and flexible options, raising escalation risks.

title: Ravi Mohan halts films: Actor vows break till divorce ends; fans express sympathy
content: Actor Ravi Mohan has broken his silence on his divorce from Aarti, addressing rumors linking singer Keneeshaa Francis to their separation. He emotionally stated his silence was not weakness and vowed to not act until his divorce is finalized. Mohan also expressed pain over not being allowed to see his children.

title: NEET paper leak: NSUI members protest outside NTA office in Delhi
content: NSUI protested against the NTA over the alleged NEET-UG 2026 paper leak, demanding the agency's ban. The CBI is investigating a larger conspiracy, with accused Dhananjay Lokhande remanded to custody to identify linked NTA officials. Investigations reveal financial transactions and circulation of leaked papers among multiple accused.

title: 'She cheated on me, gave our son to someone else': Hyderabad man dies by suicide, uploads video
content: A 28-year-old cab driver died by suicide in Hayathnagar, with police registering an abetment to suicide case against a woman named Saritha. The deceased's mother alleged his death was due to Saritha's betrayal after promising marriage. A suicide note and video recordings indicated he believed Saritha had abandoned their son and married another man.

title: Scary! Pakistan cricketer leaves field on stretcher after head injury - Watch
content: Pakistan's Hasan Ali suffered a head injury after a nasty blow while attempting a return catch on day one of the second Test against Bangladesh. He was taken off on a stretcher but thankfully returned to the field later. Earlier, Pakistan's seamers had a strong start, taking early wickets.

title: Revisiting Kargil: How India crushed Musharraf's bid to cut off Ladakh
content: In 1999, Pakistan's army, led by General Musharraf, infiltrated Kargil, aiming to sever India's vital National Highway-1A and seize the Siachen Glacier. Local shepherds alerted the Indian Army, which initially mistook intruders for terrorists. After a patrol led by Lt. Saurabh Kalia was captured and brutally killed, the full scale of the incursion became clear.

title: Defence boost for Pakistan: PKR 100 billion military hike on cards in IMF-backed budget
content: Pakistan plans to increase its defense budget by PKR 100 billion for the next fiscal year, reaching an estimated PKR 2.665 trillion. This move aligns with an IMF-backed reform program projecting a significant rise in federal revenues to PKR 17.144 trillion. The government is also committed to digitizing payments and tackling corruption across institutions.

title: 'Not easy to replace Rohit Sharma': Ashwin on Pandya captaincy and MI struggles
content: Mumbai Indians have endured a disappointing IPL 2026, failing to reach the playoffs after just four wins in 12 matches. Captain Hardik Pandya has faced criticism for his batting and leadership, but Ravichandran Ashwin defended him, stating it's unfair to solely blame the captain when the team has underperformed collectively.

title: 'If it continues to harbour terrorists ...': Army chief's stern warning to Pakistan
content: Army chief Upendra Dwivedi issued a stark warning to Pakistan, stating that continued harboring of terrorists and operations against India would force them to "decide whether they want to be part of geography or history." This statement comes after the first anniversary of Operation Sindoor, where Indian forces conducted precision strikes on terror infrastructure.

title: 'Who paid for your globetrotting?': BJP claims Rahul took 54 foreign trips in 22 yrs
content: The BJP has accused Rahul Gandhi of a significant financial discrepancy, alleging his foreign travels over two decades cost approximately Rs 60 crore, far exceeding his declared income. The party questions potential Foreign Contribution Regulation Act and tax law violations, demanding transparency regarding the source of funds for his numerous international trips.

title: ‘Ban these chits!’: Rayudu calls Akash Singh viral ‘chit’ celebration 'rubbish'
content: Akash Singh's IPL 2026 debut for Lucknow Super Giants was marked by a unique celebration involving a self-written paper chit after taking crucial wickets. While the gesture went viral, former CSK batter Ambati Rayudu criticized it as 'rubbish' and likened it to using cheat notes in an exam.

title: ‘Will erode goodwill’: Congress warns Vijay against inducting rebel AIADMK MLAs
content: Congress has cautioned Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay against incorporating rebel AIADMK members into his government.  Karti Chidambaram stressed that the public mandate was for a government independent of both AIADMK and DMK.  Inducting the splinter group, he warned, would significantly damage Vijay's goodwill and credibility, urging him to remain true to the electoral mandate.

title: Wholesale prices are up — your bills could be next
content: Indian consumers may soon face higher prices as wholesale inflation surges, driven by global conflicts impacting commodity and energy costs. While retail inflation remains soft for now, a widening gap between wholesale and consumer price indices suggests businesses will likely pass on increased production expenses, leading to costlier everyday goods and services in the coming months.

title: 'Names don't determine success': Prasidh explains secret behind Gujarat Titans' resurgence
content: After a stuttering start to IPL 2026, Gujarat Titans have emerged as one of the tournament’s most complete sides, with a relentless bowling attack led by Kagiso Rabada, Mohammed Siraj, Prasidh Krishna. Speaking to the Teamofindia.com, Prasidh opens up on GT’s turnaround, the team’s hard-length bowling philosophy, Shubman Gill’s calm leadership and his own evolution after last season’s Purple Cap-winning campaign.

title: India-Brics trade booms to $416 billion, but deficit keeps rising
content: India's trade with BRICS countries surged over five years, with imports growing significantly faster than exports. This has led to a near doubling of India's trade deficit with the bloc, now reaching $224 billion, primarily driven by increased purchases from Russia and China.

title: Gold chain snatched from Bihar MLA Rahul Kumar’s brother during morning walk; watch
content: In a shocking midday heist, two masked individuals on a motorcycle swiftly snatched a gold necklace valued at around Rs 3 lakh from Rohit Kumar, the sibling of Jehanabad's MLA Rahul Kumar, in the bustling Kankarbagh area of Patna.

title: Devotees offer prayers at Bhojshala complex; how principles from Ayodhya verdict paved way
content: Madhya Pradesh High Court has declared the Bhojshala complex in Dhar a Hindu temple and Sanskrit learning center, granting exclusive daily worship rights to the Hindu community. The court overturned a 2003 ASI order restricting Hindu prayers. The Union government and ASI will manage the site, with the Muslim community offered alternative land for a mosque.

title: Hockey India removes support staff member over sexual misconduct allegations
content: Jharkhand government removed hockey coach Sudhir Golla from the Eklavya Hockey Training Centre in Ranchi due to absenteeism and harassment complaints from girl trainees. Hockey India also removed him from an U-18 boys' camp in Bhopal following the allegations. This incident highlights ongoing concerns about misconduct in Indian hockey's grassroots setups.

title: 'ISIS greatly diminished': Trump announces killing of 'most active terrorist' in Africa
content: US President Donald Trump announced the killing of Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, identified as ISIS's global second-in-command, in a joint US-Nigerian operation. Trump stated the mission, executed at his direction, successfully removed a key terrorist leader who was planning attacks on Americans and destabilizing Africa. This operation significantly weakens ISIS's global network.

title: Biryani on a budget: Why your next meal may cost more?
content: Get ready for pricier meals as India's restaurants and delivery apps plan a 5-10% food cost increase from next week. Soaring fuel prices, coupled with LPG shortages and staff issues, are forcing businesses to pass on rising transportation, packaging, and input expenses. This move disrupts annual pricing cycles, impacting dining out and delivery costs.

title: 'Calling to molest again': Lucknow professor offers to leak exam paper to girl student
content: Lucknow University's zoology assistant professor is in police custody following allegations of leaking exam papers and making inappropriate advances towards a female student. Audio recordings, submitted by the student, reportedly capture the professor discussing paper leaks and offering academic favours in exchange for meetings. The university initiated an internal probe before police registered an FIR.

title: Unable to pay Rs 2k rent, Gujarat man lets landlord, aide repeatedly rape wife and daughter
content: A disturbing incident in Morbi, Gujarat, has surfaced where a tenant allegedly permitted his landlord and a relative to repeatedly assault his wife and 13-year-old daughter due to unpaid rent. The wife's mother filed a complaint, leading to the landlord's arrest and judicial custody. Police are searching for absconding relatives involved in the heinous crime.

title: Op Epic Fury to resume? Trump weighs fresh Iran strikes after China visit: Report
content: As US President Trump returns from China, pressure mounts over Iran. Top aides are reportedly preparing options for renewed military action against Tehran if diplomacy falters, including intensified bombing and potential Special Operations deployments.  Iran, meanwhile, signals readiness for conflict, while the Strait of Hormuz remains a critical concern for global oil prices.

title: 4-year-old pulled out safely from borewell after 9-hour rescue operation in Punjab
content: A four-year-old boy was safely rescued from a newly dug borewell in Hoshiarpur, Punjab, after a challenging nine-hour operation. Multiple rescue agencies and local volunteers collaborated to pull the child out from a depth of 20 to 30 feet. The boy, who appeared stable after the rescue, was immediately taken to a hospital for examination.

title: Ride collapses at ‘Disneyland fair’ in Bihar’s Sasaram; several children, women injured
content: A ride at a 'Disneyland fair' in Sasaram, Bihar, collapsed on Friday, resulting in injuries to several children and women. The injured were promptly taken to Sadar Hospital for treatment. Authorities are investigating the cause of the incident and the exact number of casualties is yet to be confirmed.

title: 'Expected to score 650 marks': 22-year-old NEET aspirant dies by suicide
content: A 22-year-old NEET aspirant died by suicide in Sikar, with his family attributing his death to severe stress following the NEET exam cancellation due to a paper leak. Another 20-year-old MBBS aspirant was found dead in Lakhimpur Kheri, with his family also linking it to the NEET exam's cancellation and rescheduling, though police are investigating personal reasons.

title: Meet Manoj Kumar Sharma, '12th Fail' Fame IPS Officer Who Got Key Mumbai Police Role
content: A 2005-batch IPS officer, Manoj Kumar Sharma rose to national prominence after his life story inspired the blockbuster film 12th Fail.

title: Poll Body Orders Probe After Foreigners Allegedly Vote In Tamil Nadu Elections
content: The EC's action follows a report submitted by the police recently after they arrested ten foreign nationals who attempted to board a flight.

title: Indian Mangoes Make Fresh Entry In Seattle After Year-Long Outreach Efforts
content: The renewed spotlight on Indian mangoes came after a special segment aired on Fox 13 News' "Good Day Seattle", where India's Consul General in Seattle, Prakash Gupta, spoke about the arrival

title: 2,000-Year-Old Egyptian Mummy Found With Ancient Greek War Poem: Report
content: The discovery was made at Oxyrhynchus, an ancient burial site in central Egypt, by a team from the University of Barcelona

title: 'Leaked Papers For You, Darling': Lucknow Professor Told Student, Arrested
content: The recording of calls to a woman student at the Lucknow University brought to the fore her continued harassment by an assistant professor in the name of leaked examination papers.

title: Kerala Leadership Issue Resolved, Congress' Next Challenge: Karnataka
content: While Siddaramaiah has been pushing for a cabinet reshuffle and the filling up of vacant ministerial positions, Shivakumar continues to nurture ambitions of taking over as chief minister during the current term.

title: "If Pak Continues To Operate Against India...": Army Chief's Big Warning
content: General Dwivedi's remarks, though brief, carried a blunt message to Pakistan and reiterated India's stand against terrorism.

title: YouTube, Snap Settle School District's Social Media Addiction Claims
content: Alphabet's YouTube and Snap have reached settlements in the first case set for trial in litigation seeking to force social media platforms to

title: Putin To Visit China On May 19-20 After Trump Trip
content: Russian President Vladimir Putin will travel to China on May 19 for a two-day visit, hot on the heels of US President Donald Trump's trip to...

title: C Naidu Moves Party's Key Event To Hybrid Format After PM Modi's Appeal
content: The decision was taken after Chandrababu Naidu held a teleconference with ministers, senior leaders and party functionaries on Friday.

title: Scientists Find Climate Change Is Reducing Oxygen In Rivers Worldwide
content: Warmer water, which happens with human-caused climate change, releases more oxygen into the atmosphere.

title: Ex-Merchant Navy Officer Sent Indian "Cyber Slaves" To 2 Nations, Arrested
content: According to the police, the ex-merchant navy officer - identified as Nagesh Kumar - is the international cyber slavery gang's main leader.

title: Massive Fire At Shop In J&K's Akhnoor, Items Worth Lakhs Destroyed
content: Flames spread rapidly through the shop, which was stocked with paint, chemicals and other inflammable hardware material.

title: Robert Vadra Gets Pre-Arrest Bail In Money Laundering Case
content: As per the order of the Rouse Avenue Court, Vadra was granted bail on furnishing a bail bond of Rs 50,000 along with one surety of the like amount.

title: Who Was Abu-Bilal, Top ISIS Leader Killed In Joint US-Nigeria Op
content: US President Donald Trump said on Friday that Abu-Bilal al-Minuki had been eliminated in a carefully planned mission.

content: The interaction was interrupted multiple times by a journalist who was allegedly speaking on the phone while Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov was answering questions.

title: Ex-Imam Faked Spiritual Powers To Rape Girls In London, Gets Life Sentence
content: Abdul Halim Khan pretended to have spiritual powers to coerce and sexually abuse girls as young as 12, a Metropolitan Police investigation revealed.

title: US says El Nino just weeks away, earlier than expected
content: forecasts from international agencies suggest El Nino could be weeks away . it’s now likely that the weather condition will prevail during most of the four-month monsoon period as opposed to previous expectations that it’ll develop in the second half of the season .

title: 'They will take out a gun': Russia's Lavrov warns journalist on phone during Delhi presser - watch
content: a video of the incident has circulated widely on social media . lavrov initially asked the journalist to leave the conference room . but when the call continued, he issued a sharper warning . he said the phone should be put away or security personnel would take the gun out .

title: Stay clear of Aravalis till we decide its definition, says SC
content: apex court said not an inch of Aravali would be allowed to be used for mining till an expert committee, to be constituted by the court, redefined what constitutes aravali hills and ranges . the process for renewal and grant of leases could proceed without their finalisation, a bench said .
